exus.help Aug 29 2016 CONTENTS: Simics and Flexus Installation Notes Licenses Simics and Flexus ----------------- Simics (http://www.simics.com) is a software development tool that simulates an embedded system in a virtual enviro

imulates an embedded system in a virtual environment. Flexus (http://www.ece.cmu.edu/~simflex/) is a set of tools that run with Simics to simulate multiprocessor systems running unmodified operating systems and applications. Thes

d operating systems and applications. These programs wee installed for CS152 students in Spring 2010 on Linux computers in 125 Cory. Now, it may run on these Centos servers: hpse-9.eecs.berkeley.edu ... hpse-15.eecs.berkeley.edu The p

erkeley.edu ... hpse-15.eecs.berkeley.edu The program is in /share/instsww/pkg/virtutech/simics-3.0.30/bin/simics All users with Instructional UNIX accounts can 'ssh' into those servers (http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/newusers.html).

//inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/newusers.html). Additional image files (from https://www.simics.net/mwf/vh?34) installed for CS152 include simics-3.0.30/targets/x86-440bx/images/tango1-fedora5.craff simics-3.0.30/targets/ia64-460gx/images/va

.craff simics-3.0.30/targets/ia64-460gx/images/vasa5-rh72.craff simics-3.0.30/targets/sunfire/images/bagle5-suse73.craff Note that both CS152 and CS250 have adopted "chisel", which is a Berkeley project that they tend to use along with Synops

ey project that they tend to use along with Synopsys tools (https://chisel.eecs.berkeley.edu/). Installation Notes ------------------ Simics requires GCC 4.1.2/GLIBC 2.5-18 to compile. Licenses -------- Simics has free academic licenses,
es -------- Simics has free academic licenses, and Flexus is free. Instructional Support Group inst@eecs.berkeley.edu (378,384,386 Cory, 333 Soda)
